---
title: "Rust Practice Tools"
subtitle: "Practice Rust in Browser and Locally"
---

## 🎯 Two Friendly Ways to Practice Rust

### Option 1: Rust Playground (Fastest Start)

The official Rust Playground is the fastest way to run Rust with no installation.

<div style="text-align: center; margin: 2rem 0;">
  <a href="https://play.rust-lang.org/" class="btn btn-primary btn-lg" style="padding: 1rem 2rem; font-size: 1.2rem; text-decoration: none; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #6366f1, #ec4899); border-radius: 0.5rem; color: white; display: inline-block;">
    🦀 Open Rust Playground
  </a>
</div>

**Features:**
- ✨ Instant compilation and run
- 🔄 Edit and run code repeatedly
- 📎 Share code by URL
- 🌐 No installation or account required

### Option 2: Local Rust Toolchain (Best for Growth)

Install Rust once and build everything with Cargo.

```bash
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
rustc --version
cargo --version
```

Then create and run your first project:

```bash
cargo new hello_rust
cd hello_rust
cargo run
```

## 📚 Lesson Notebooks in This Project

This Rust Quest includes notebooks for Quests 1-19:

1. [Quest 1 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/01-variables.ipynb)
2. [Quest 2 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/02-data-types.ipynb)
3. [Quest 3 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/03-conditionals.ipynb)
4. [Quest 4 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/04-loops.ipynb)
5. [Quest 5 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/05-lists.ipynb)
6. [Quest 6 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/06-functions.ipynb)
7. [Quest 7 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/07-dictionaries.ipynb)
8. [Quest 8 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/08-fibonacci.ipynb)
9. [Quest 9 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/09-sorting.ipynb)
10. [Quest 10 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/10-number-game.ipynb)
11. [Quest 11 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/11-recursion.ipynb)
12. [Quest 12 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/12-complexity.ipynb)
13. [Quest 13 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/13-iterative-loops.ipynb)
14. [Quest 14 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/14-functions-non-returning.ipynb)
15. [Quest 15 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/15-functions-returning.ipynb)
16. [Quest 16 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/16-lambda-functions.ipynb)
17. [Quest 17 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/17-higher-order-functions.ipynb)
18. [Quest 18 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/18-decorators.ipynb)
19. [Quest 19 Notebook](jupyterlite/content/19-classes.ipynb)

Each notebook includes Rust examples and gentle challenge prompts. Since JupyterLite typically runs Python kernels, treat the Rust cells as guided reading/practice snippets and run them in Rust Playground or local Rust.

## 💡 Tips for First-Time Rust Learners

- Read the compiler error message all the way through.
- Start tiny: print values, then add one feature at a time.
- Use `println!("{:?}", value)` for quick debugging.
- Keep one browser tab open to Rust Playground.

## 🌐 Works Great on Student Devices

All content is web-based and readable on laptops, tablets, and phones. For actually running Rust code, prefer the Rust Playground link.

---

<div style="text-align: center; padding: 2rem;">
  <a href="https://play.rust-lang.org/" style="font-size: 1.5rem; text-decoration: none;">
    🦀 **Open Rust Playground Now** →
  </a>
</div>
